The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the IRA's Production Tax Credits (45Y). The OBBBA harmonizes domestic content requirements under Section 48E with those under Section 45Y for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48Ε) have been made mandatory. President Trump signed H.R.1, the 'One Big Beautiful Bill Act,' on July 4, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits and imposes specific domestic content percentages for Section 48E projects. These requirements are now mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility.

The House of Representatives passed a reconciliation bill (H.R. 1) on May 22, 2025, which amends several Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) tax credits, including the Advanced Manufacturing Production Credit (Section 45X). The bill introduces Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions that would require domestic materials or subcomponents for certain credits, taking effect under specific conditions.
